hypotheses.text: A multi-feature eye-tracking protocol combining fixation-duration variability, saccadic latency, and blink rate can achieve ≥80% sensitivity and ≥75% specificity for ADHD screening in clinical settings.
hypotheses.rationale: Single eye-tracking metrics have shown moderate diagnostic accuracy; combining multiple features may yield clinically acceptable performance. A feasibility score of 0.65 indicates reasonable practical implementation potential.

raw_papers.title: Eye-tracking based attention assessment in children with ADHD: A feasibility study
raw_papers.abstract: A feasibility study examining the use of a 15-minute eye-tracking protocol in 30 children diagnosed with ADHD. Results showed that 93% of children completed the protocol successfully, with high test-retest reliability (ICC = 0.85) for fixation duration measures.
